findings.summary: Unit 3-A's concrete OMP tile edit does not match the current wizard tile contract and misses the keyboard-navigation update required by a seventh tile.
findings.evidence: plan.md:893-902 proposes { id, name, description, icon, requiresSetup, setupUrl }. src/renderer/components/Wizard/screens/AgentSelectionScreen.tsx:31-36 defines AgentTile as { id, name, supported, description, brandColor? }. src/renderer/components/Wizard/screens/AgentSelectionScreen.tsx:91-93 hardcodes GRID_ROWS = 2, and lines 599-603 bound ArrowDown navigation by GRID_ROWS.
findings.impact: Implementing the plan literally will not match the current tile contract and would leave the added tile outside the declared keyboard-navigation grid.
findings.recommended_fix: Rewrite Unit 3-A so the OMP tile uses the real AgentTile fields (supported, optional brandColor) and explicitly updates grid navigation to handle seven tiles, ideally by deriving row count from AGENT_TILES.length.

findings.summary: Unit 3-B's proposed OMP command list does not match OMP's actual slash-command sources.
findings.evidence: plan.md:922-930 lists /plan, /plan-new, /compact, /mcp, /thinking, /background, /commit, /worktree, and /role. packages/coding-agent/src/slash-commands/builtin-registry.ts contains /plan (104-109), /mcp (435-463), /compact (500-508), and /background (550-554), but no builtin entries for plan-new, thinking, commit, worktree, or role. Supplemental evidence: agent/commands/plan-new.md:8-15 defines /plan-new as a file-based command, agent/extensions/implementation-engine/index.ts:3276-3278 registers /worktree, packages/coding-agent/CHANGELOG.md:5420 records /thinking being replaced by /settings, and packages/coding-agent/src/cli.ts:48-50 exposes commit as a CLI subcommand rather than a builtin slash command.
findings.impact: Maestro would advertise commands that are not backed by the builtin registry the plan cites, producing incorrect autocomplete and misleading integration work.
findings.recommended_fix: Replace Unit 3-B's command inventory with commands from the actual OMP discovery path Maestro will use, and distinguish builtin-registry commands from file-based or extension-registered commands.
